title: CI: Fix directive `//nolint:revive` is unused for linter "revive" on ARM
body:
This PR fixes `golangci-lint` on ARM64 machines. 
Fixes:
- `directive `//nolint:revive` is unused for linter "revive" (nolintlint)`

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: thanks for taking care of this ... i hit it the other day and had it on my list of things to do ...

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude*, *Honny1*, *kolyshkin* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
It would also be nice to add a linter run for arm (can't do it on intel hw because of cgo). Perhaps it can be shoved into the `build_aarch64_task`?

> It would also be nice to add a linter run for arm (can't do it on intel hw because of cgo). Perhaps it can be shoved into the build_aarch64_task?

Timing is critical, that may be enough to push the aarch build task to be the slowest one and thus making overall CI time slower. The validate task should really be used for that.
Overall our cross builds are able to build without CGO so why can we not cross lint with CGO disabled? Sure we loose the ability to lint some cgo specific files but that is just a small thing and they are likely already covered in the normal run anyway.

(I wonder how source file linting ended up being OS/architecture specific..., and the fact that we need to lint with all different build tags certainly does not help)


With 
```diff
diff --git a/libpod/lock/shm/shm_lock_test.go b/libpod/lock/shm/shm_lock_test.go
index 532d50aaef..9949b5dfcf 100644
--- a/libpod/lock/shm/shm_lock_test.go
+++ b/libpod/lock/shm/shm_lock_test.go
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
-//go:build linux || freebsd
+//go:build cgo && (linux || freebsd)
 
 package shm
 
```

I am able to run
```
$ CGO_ENABLED=0 GOARCH=arm64 bin/golangci-lint run --build-tags "containers_image_openpgp exclude_graphdriver_btrfs exclude_graphdriver_overlay"
test/e2e/config_arm64.go:6:163: directive `//nolint:revive` is unused for linter "revive" (nolintlint)
        STORAGE_FS          = "overlay"                                                                                                                                  //nolint:revive
...
```
So it can work.
@kolyshkin, will you create a PR to enable the linter on ARM?
